definition: Lasting for only a very short time.
A fortunate discovery made entirely by chance.
The pleasant smell of rain falling on dry earth.
Too great or extreme to be expressed in words.
Perfectly clear and transparent, like calm water nothing disturbs.
Wildly idealistic and gloriously impractical.
Seeming to be present everywhere at once.
Calm, peaceful and happy, recalling an idyllic time now gone.
Fluent and persuasive in speech or writing.
The ability to recover quickly from difficulty.
Giving off or reflecting a soft, bright light.
A sentimental longing for the past.
A typical example or model of something.
Delicate and light in a way that seems unworldly.
No longer in use; out of date.
A sudden and striking moment of realization.
Sparing and economical with money or resources.
Using far more words than are needed.
Reserved in nature and saying very little.
A supposed remedy for every problem or disease.
A difficult, tangled situation hard to escape.
Clear and easy to understand; thinking sharply.
The highest point reached by something.
The lowest point of a situation or fortune.
Open, honest, and sincere expression.
Full of quiet, yearning sadness.
Drawn from a broad and diverse range of sources.
Holding firmly; persistent and determined.
Producing strong, clear images in the mind.
Comfort or consolation in a time of distress.
Sweet and smooth, pleasing to hear.
Acting with one's true identity concealed.
The soft fading light just after the sun has slipped below the horizon.
A steady calm of mind that holds firm even when life turns stormy.
The rise and fall that gives speech or marching feet their flow.
A small recess set back from the main room, made for quiet retreat.
Where a river widens and meets the salt of the open sea.
A pensive, lingering sorrow with no single cause you can name.
A tale whose surface hides a deeper lesson beneath its characters.
A shoulder bag a schoolchild once carried books and lunch in.
The wispy, feathery streaks drawn high across a clear blue dome.
Prone to childish sulks and snappish little fits over trifles.
Writings of doubtful origin, left outside the trusted canon.
A fine writing surface prepared from treated animal skin.
Describing rich soil left behind by the slow work of flowing water.
Cheerfully confident that things will turn out for the best.
The closing passage that rounds off a piece after its main argument.
The low protective wall running along the edge of a roof or bridge.
Faintly salty, as where freshwater and tidal currents quietly mingle.
Sullen, gloomy, and disinclined to say a kind word to anyone.
A recurring thread of melody or image that ties a work together.
A criss-crossed frame of strips that climbing plants love to scale.
Lush and richly green with the vigour of new growth.
Carrying a tone of soft, mournful pleading that tugs at the heart.
A work that lovingly imitates the manner of another artist or era.
The strip beneath a doorway you step over to enter a room.
The leafy canopy that crowns a forest in summer.
An intense glowing heat of feeling or belief that drives a person on.
A brief, vivid scene that captures a mood without a full plot.
To wander idly along a winding, unhurried path with no fixed aim.
A single soft segment of colour unfurling on a bloom.
A bitter, lingering ill will that festers long after the quarrel.
An instrumental composition built from contrasting movements.
A craftsman who mends and stitches worn footwear by hand.
A vast treeless plain where the ground stays frozen most of the year.
Untroubled and clear, like a lake at dawn with no ripple on it.
A solemn musical setting composed to honour the dead.
A small ornament of little value but often dear to its owner.
A wide grassy plain dotted with scattered trees under a hot sun.
Sunk so low in spirits that hope itself seems out of reach.
The whole body of work an artist leaves behind.
A pleasant state of being lost in one's own daydreams.
An open field of grass and wildflowers humming with insects.
Pouring out warmth and praise far beyond what restraint would allow.
An ornate style fond of drama, curves and lavish ornament.
A portable case of glass that shelters a flame against the wind.
A slow river of ancient ice grinding its way down a valley.
Spoiling for a fight and eager to bristle at the slightest provocation.
A frothy, decorative style of swirls, shells and pastel charm.
A rounded paving stone that makes an old street rattle underwheel.
A deep, dangerous split that opens silently in a sheet of ice.
So unsure of oneself that one shrinks from stepping forward.
A wall painting laid into plaster while it is still damp.
A feather trimmed to a point and dipped in ink for writing.
The heap of rubble a retreating ice mass leaves scattered behind.
Easy to talk to, warm of manner, and pleasant in any company.
The bold play of light against shadow in a painted scene.
An openwork frame of crossed slats that supports trailing vines.
A long steep slope where the land suddenly drops to a lower plain.
Brimming with hearty good humor that spreads to everyone nearby.
Thick ridges of paint piled up to catch the eye and the light.
A pale, translucent stone carvers prize for its smooth, milky glow.
A high point of land that juts boldly out over the water.
Silently bad-tempered, with a cloud sitting heavy on the brow.
A frozen, picture-like arrangement of figures held in a pose.
A covered walkway around a courtyard where monks once paced in silence.
A narrow neck of land linking two larger bodies together.
Watchful and on guard, slow to trust until trust is earned.
A miniature scene staged in three dimensions for viewing.
The stone floor of a fireplace, long the warm centre of a home.
A scattered chain of islands strewn across the open water.
Genuinely sorry, and humbled by the wrong one has done.
A painting split across three hinged panels meant to stand together.
The square block on which a statue or column firmly stands.
A shallow stretch of calm water held back from the sea by a reef or bar.
Calm and unruffled, content to let the day drift gently by.
A sequence of swift images cut together to suggest passing time.
The upper chamber of a tower where bells are hung to ring.
A deep inlet carved between towering cliffs by long-vanished ice.
Quick to flare into temper at the smallest spark of annoyance.
A stately hymn tune sung in harmony by many voices.
A heavy woven hanging picturing scenes in coloured thread.
A small sheltered curve of shoreline tucked away from the open waves.
Generous and forgiving, especially toward a beaten rival.
An intricate part-song for several unaccompanied voices.
A smith who shapes and nails iron shoes onto horses' hooves.
A lesser stream that feeds its water into a greater river.
Fawning and overeager to please, bowing far too low to flatter.
The written words that an opera's singers actually perform.
A branched fixture of lights and glass hung from a high ceiling.
Water tumbling in stages down a steep run of rock.
Lost in deep, faintly wistful thought, gazing at nothing in particular.
The notes of a chord sounded one after another rather than at once.
A low, soft, continuous sound that is hard to make out.
A tiny thread of running water trickling over the ground.
A sharp, stinging bitterness that sours words between people.
A gradual swelling of sound toward a forceful peak.
Something so fine and delicate it seems woven from spider silk.
A violent, rushing surge of water after heavy rain.
Disposed to do good and wish others well, with a warm hand open.
The distinct colour that lets you tell a flute from a violin.
To stroll along in a relaxed, leisurely and unhurried manner.
A small whirl where the current circles back on itself.
Given to sudden whims, changing one's mind on the lightest breeze.
The line or verse that returns again at each chorus.
A heat-proof vessel in which metals are melted, or a severe trial.
A submerged bank of sand that lurks just below the surface.
Bearing pain or hardship without a flicker of complaint.
A fourteen-line poem bound by a fixed rhyming pattern.
A small entrance hall lying just inside the outer door.
Low soggy ground where reeds stand in shallow standing water.
Hungry to repay a slight with a wound of equal or greater size.
A mournful poem written in memory of someone lost.
A tall, tapering four-sided pillar that ends in a small pyramid.
The fan of silty channels where a river splays out into the sea.
Friendly and good-natured, the sort everyone is glad to meet.
A simple narrative poem, often sung, that tells a folk tale.
A footed ceremonial cup raised during a sacred rite.
Open windswept ground covered in low shrubs and coarse plants.
The hollow state in which every door seems shut and bolted.
A grouped set of lines forming one block of a poem.
A restless craving to set off and roam distant places.
A bleak upland expanse of rough grass and heather.
A soaring rush of bliss that lifts one clear off the ground.
The measured beat that gives a line of verse its pulse.
Delicate ornamental work of fine twisted gold or silver wire.
A dense tangle of bushes and small trees hard to push through.
A trembling unease at what may be waiting just ahead.
A single metrical line, or poetry as opposed to prose.
To patch or put something together roughly and hastily.
A small cluster of trees standing apart in open country.
The gift of feeling another's pain as if it were quietly your own.
Ordinary written language, free of rhyme and strict measure.
A crude likeness of a person, often made to be burned in protest.
A sunny clearing where the forest opens to let in the light.
A flat indifference in which nothing stirs you to care.
A descriptive nickname fixed to a person or thing by habit.
A clerk who copied out documents by hand before printing spread.
The leafy roof formed by the crowns of the tallest trees.
Burning with eager, passionate devotion to a cause or a person.
A short, sharp saying that ends with a clever sting.
The low tangle of plants crowding the floor beneath the trees.
Left lonely and abandoned, stripped of comfort and of hope.
A pithy statement of truth packed into very few words.
A wide-mouthed pitcher once used to carry water for washing.
The thick main limbs reaching out from the trunk of a great tree.
Thriving in company and forever seeking the warmth of a crowd.
A short rule of conduct offered as general wisdom.
The chosen state of being pleasantly alone and undisturbed.
A slender coiling shoot a climbing plant uses to grip its support.
Holding one's thoughts close, parting with words only sparingly.
A homely old saying that passes down folk wisdom.
A thin writing material scraped smooth from the skin of a sheep.
The long divided leaf of a fern or palm arching gracefully.
Overflowing with triumphant delight, ready to shout it aloud.
A time-worn saying accepted by many as plain truth.
To leap and skip about playfully, as a young lamb does.
One of the green outer parts cradling a bud before it opens.
Marked by a dry, scornful wit that mocks beneath a thin smile.
A starting truth taken as self-evident without proof.
A tradesman who builds barrels and casks from curved wooden staves.
The sweet liquid a flower offers to lure bees and birds.
A tender ache to ease the suffering you see in another.
Wild exaggeration used for effect, not to be taken literally.
An ornament hung from a chain so it rests against the chest.
The fine golden dust a bloom releases to fertilise another.
Stung into anger by something felt to be deeply unjust.
A figure that calls one thing another to reveal a likeness.
A subtle shade of meaning or feeling easily overlooked.
The mass of delicate flowers that clothes a fruit tree in spring.
Pleasantly slow and drowsy, with no wish to stir or hurry.
A comparison that openly likens one thing to another.
A long row of evenly spaced pillars holding up a roof or arches.
A modified leaf, often vividly coloured, that sits below a flower.
Hostile and combative, carrying a chip squarely on the shoulder.
A phrase yoking two words that seem to contradict each other.
A tall one-handled drinking mug, often with a hinged lid.
An underground stem that creeps sideways and sends up new shoots.
Bubbling over with high spirits that simply will not be contained.
A statement that seems self-contradicting yet may hold true.
A deep, persistent yearning for something out of reach.
A crusty growth of fungus and alga clinging to bare rock and bark.
Touched by a habitual gentle sadness that colors one's whole outlook.
Saying one thing while plainly meaning its opposite.
An artisan who fits feathers to arrows so they fly true.
A soft green cushion of tiny plants carpeting damp stone.
Acting on sudden impulse, headlong, with no pause for second thought.
Writing that mocks folly and vice to shame or correct it.
A small picturesque cave, sometimes shaped for ornament in a garden.
A spore-bearing organism that thrives in damp shade without any leaves.
Cheerful and kindly, radiating an easy, welcoming warmth.
A comic imitation that exaggerates a work to ridicule it.
A swinging metal vessel that wafts fragrant smoke through a church.
The microscopic seed-like grain by which a mushroom spreads.
Drawn with unhealthy fascination toward gloom, death, and decay.
A biting piece of writing meant to ridicule a person.
A flow of tears overspilling the eye, or a rhetorical repetition.
The hidden web of fine threads from which mushrooms arise.
Fearless in the face of danger, pressing on where others falter.
A fierce written attack on an opinion or belief.
A railing held up by a row of small shaped posts along a stair.
The tunnelled hollow a small animal digs to shelter underground.
One who flatters the powerful shamelessly to win their favor.
A long, angry speech that denounces something bitterly.
A large vessel with a handle and spout for serving drink at table.
A maze of connected tunnels where rabbits live and breed.
Filled with deep, hushed respect bordering on awe.
The art of persuasive speaking and skilful argument.
An intense, aching wish for something or someone absent.
The full covering of feathers that clothes and colours a bird.
Forever grumbling and finding fault in a peevish, whining tone.
Clever reasoning that misleads while appearing sound.
A workshop where raw hides are cured into usable leather.
The curved, gripping claw of a hunting bird.
Free of every disturbance, peaceful and still to the core.
Subtle but often deceptive reasoning about moral questions.
A small rounded dome crowning a roof or larger dome.
A young bird that has just grown the feathers it needs to fly.
Boldly daring to the edge of recklessness, heedless of the risk.
The clash of opposing ideas pursued toward a truth.
A stoppered glass bottle into which wine is poured to breathe.
A small family group of game birds flushed together from cover.
So mean and low in conduct as to deserve open contempt.
An argument drawing a conclusion from two stated premises.
To speak very softly, using breath rather than the full voice.
The leafy nest a squirrel builds high among the branches.
Full of light laughter and merry, infectious gladness.
Formal, extended treatment of a subject in speech or writing.
An ornate container made to hold a saint's treasured remains.
The branching bony crown a stag grows and sheds each year.
Grave and earnest in manner, with no trace of frivolity.
A long, systematic written study of a single subject.
A deep, narrow gorge cut into the land by running water.
The long flowing hair along the neck of a horse or lion.
Eaten up with longing for what rightfully belongs to another.
A public declaration of a movement's aims and beliefs.
A drinking vessel with a stem and foot but no handle.
The ridge between the shoulder blades where a horse's height is measured.
A gentle, abiding fondness that warms one toward another.
An opening passage that sets the stage for what follows.
The muscular hip and upper thigh of a four-legged animal.
A slow-burning grudge nursed quietly over a perceived wrong.
An introductory section spoken or written before the main work.
A craftsman of old who built and repaired wooden wagons.
The loose fold of skin that hangs beneath the throat of cattle.
Sparkling with lively, animated energy that lights up a room.
A closing section that rounds off a story after its end.
A heavy iron grating dropped to seal a castle's gateway.
The skin of an animal still bearing its thick fur.
Easily frightened, shrinking back at the faintest shadow of threat.
A gathered collection of writings chosen from many sources.
A small charm worn on the body to ward off harm or bad luck.
The hard protective shell shielding a turtle or crab on top.
Fond of festive company, good food, and warm, flowing talk.
A concise gathering of the essentials of a larger subject.
A perfect example that embodies the very essence of its kind.
The hard case in which a caterpillar transforms into a winged creature.
Cast down in spirit, with the head bowed and the shoulders low.
The full stock of words belonging to a language or field.
A frame on which lengthwise threads are interwoven into cloth.
The wormlike young of an insect before it changes form.
Brazenly rude and disrespectful, defying every claim to courtesy.
The study of where words come from and how they changed.
A slender turret or the very highest point of achievement.
The long tube an insect uncoils to sip from a flower.
Easily soothed and willing to forgive once the heat has passed.
The everyday tongue of a place, as spoken by ordinary folk.
A flat narrow bottle slipped into a pocket for carrying drink.
The pair of slender feelers an insect waves to sense its world.
A set phrase whose meaning cannot be guessed from its words.
Quiet courage that endures pain and hardship without complaint.
The middle section of an insect to which its legs and wings attach.
Calm and unexcitable, hard to ruffle whatever the upheaval.
A regional form of a language with its own words and sounds.
A merchant of candles, or one who supplies ships with provisions.
The strong jaw an insect uses to seize and crush its food.
Stubbornly fixed in purpose, refusing to be moved an inch.
The specialised tongue of a trade, opaque to outsiders.
A fortified palace built in the Moorish style of old Spain.
The coloured ring that frames the dark centre of an eye.
Courteous and kind in a way that puts everyone at their ease.
Belonging to casual talk rather than formal writing.
A small ornamental box for keeping jewels or precious keepsakes.
The clear curved dome covering the front of the eyeball.
Weighed down by grief, with the heart heavy and the eyes dim.
A gentle phrase chosen to soften a harsh or awkward truth.
Kept secret and hidden, especially because it is not allowed.
The tough cord of tissue that anchors a muscle to a bone.
Cheekily bold and disrespectful, with no shame at the affront.
A comic slip that swaps a word for a similar-sounding wrong one.
The heavy iron block a smith hammers glowing metal against.
The firm yet springy tissue cushioning the ends of bones.
A profound inner stillness untouched by the noise around it.
A word or phrase that reads the same in both directions.
A round building or hall, usually crowned with a dome.
The soft tissue inside bones where blood cells are made.
Inclined to joke and tease, treating most things with a light heart.
A word formed by shuffling the letters of another.
A milky, waxy gemstone formed from very fine quartz crystals.
A single bone in the chain that forms the backbone.
A heavy, settled low spirit from which little can rouse one.
A poem whose first letters spell a hidden word downward.
A deep covered dish from which soup is ladled at the table.
The flat bone down the centre of the chest joining the ribs.
Strikingly generous, giving on a lavish and openhanded scale.
The art of forming letters into beautiful, deliberate strokes.
So grand and awe-inspiring it stirs both wonder and a little dread.
The slender bone bridging the shoulder to the top of the chest.
Surly and ungracious, mean-spirited in word and in deed.
The craft of arranging printed letters for clear, pleasing pages.
A seller of buttons, ribbons, thread and small sewing notions.
The tough band tying muscle to bone so a joint can move.
A buoyant lift of joy that makes the whole world feel lighter.
A document written or typed by hand before any printing.
A raised road carried across water or low, marshy ground.
The tiny pit in the skin from which a single hair grows.
In a sullen, gloomy manner, with a grudge worn on the sleeve.
An ancient book of bound leaves, replacing the rolled scroll.
A tiny hinged case worn on a chain, holding a portrait or curl of hair.
A broad back tooth built for grinding food to pulp.
A soft, protective gentleness shown toward those we hold dear.
A large old volume made of sheets folded just once.
Bold daring that borders on reckless, sometimes shocking nerve.
The part of the throat that holds the cords producing the voice.
An open, simmering enmity that bares its teeth at the rival.
Books printed in the very earliest years of the press.
A bag with handles squeezed to blow air onto a fire.
The dome of muscle below the chest that drives each breath.
Light of heart and quick to bounce back from any setback.
The scribbled notes a reader leaves in a book's edges.
A projecting part of a fortress, or a stronghold of some belief.
The flat side of the head between the ear and the forehead.
Shamefully cowardly, fleeing the danger that duty demands one face.
A note at a book's end giving its printing details.
An opaque reddish stone often polished for seals and ornaments.
